desc: After an enemy is removed, the inner bullet loop continues and another hit can increment the score again before ENEMIES.remove(e) raises an exception.
The bare except suppresses all exceptions and aborts remaining collision processing for the frame, concealing logic and programming failures.
Bullets that leave the screen are never removed, causing BULLETS and per-frame update/render costs to grow without bound.
Enemies spawn continuously and are not removed when they reach or damage the player, allowing ENEMIES and processing costs to grow without bound.
An overlapping enemy subtracts HP on every frame with no cooldown or removal, so one contact can drain roughly 60 HP per second.
last_score_check starts at -1, so score zero is treated as a new multiple-of-five score and grants 3 HP on the first frame.
Player coordinates are clamped to W and H even though they are the top-left of a 20x20 rectangle, allowing most of the player sprite to leave the screen.
Movement, spawning, firing, damage, and flag timing are measured in frames rather than elapsed time, so gameplay slows and timings change whenever 60 FPS cannot be maintained.
Simultaneous horizontal and vertical input moves the player about 41% faster diagonally because the movement vector is not normalized.

A single large loop directly mutates module-level dictionaries and lists, coupling input, simulation, collision, rendering, and lifecycle logic and making safe restart, testing, and maintenance difficult.
